concepts.title: Water and hydrogen bonding
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Water's polarity creates hydrogen bonds that give it cohesion, surface tension, and its role as the solvent of life. The bent molecular geometry is the structural root of these properties.

concepts.title: Macromolecules of life
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids are the four classes of biological macromolecules, each built from characteristic monomers via dehydration synthesis.

concepts.title: Protein structure
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Proteins fold from amino acid chains into primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary structures, and that three-dimensional shape determines their function.

concepts.title: Enzymes and activation energy
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Enzymes are protein catalysts that lower the activation energy of reactions by binding substrates at an active site, speeding up the chemistry of life.

concepts.title: Cell theory and cell types
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: All living things are made of cells; prokaryotic cells lack a nucleus and membrane-bound organelles, while eukaryotic cells have both.

concepts.title: Eukaryotic cell organelles
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The eukaryotic cell is organized into specialized organelles such as the nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus, and chloroplasts that each carry out distinct functions.

concepts.title: Cell membrane structure
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The plasma membrane is a fluid phospholipid bilayer studded with proteins, a structure that controls what enters and leaves the cell.

concepts.title: Cell specialization and organization
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Cells differentiate into specialized types that organize into tissues, organs, and organ systems, building the hierarchy of multicellular life.

concepts.title: Diffusion and osmosis
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Passive transport moves substances down their concentration gradient; osmosis is the diffusion of water across a semipermeable membrane, and it shapes cell volume in different solutions.

concepts.title: Active transport
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Active transport uses ATP energy to move substances against their concentration gradient through membrane pumps such as the sodium-potassium pump.

concepts.title: Photosynthesis
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Photosynthesis captures light energy in chloroplasts to convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen, through the light reactions and the Calvin cycle.

concepts.title: Cellular respiration
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Cellular respiration breaks down glucose to release energy stored as ATP, through glycolysis, the Krebs cycle, and the electron transport chain in mitochondria.

concepts.title: ATP and the energy cycle
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: ATP is the cell's energy currency, storing and releasing energy as it cycles between ATP and ADP to power cellular work.

concepts.title: The cell cycle
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The cell cycle is the ordered sequence of interphase and mitotic phases that a cell passes through as it grows, copies its DNA, and divides.

concepts.title: Mitosis
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Mitosis divides a cell's duplicated chromosomes into two genetically identical daughter nuclei through prophase, metaphase, anaphase, and telophase.

concepts.title: Meiosis
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Meiosis is a two-stage division that produces four genetically diverse haploid gametes, with crossing over and independent assortment generating variation.

concepts.title: Mendelian genetics and Punnett squares
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Mendel's laws of segregation and independent assortment predict how dominant and recessive alleles are inherited, modeled with Punnett squares.

concepts.title: Non-Mendelian inheritance
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Patterns such as incomplete dominance, codominance, multiple alleles, and polygenic traits extend beyond simple dominant-recessive inheritance.

concepts.title: Sex linkage and pedigrees
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Genes on sex chromosomes show sex-linked inheritance patterns, and pedigrees trace how traits pass through generations of a family.

concepts.title: DNA structure
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: DNA is a double helix of two antiparallel nucleotide strands held together by complementary base pairing between A-T and G-C.

concepts.title: DNA replication
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: DNA replication is the semiconservative copying of DNA, in which helicase unwinds the helix and DNA polymerase builds new complementary strands.

concepts.title: Transcription and translation
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Gene expression flows from DNA to RNA to protein: transcription copies a gene into mRNA, and translation reads mRNA codons at the ribosome to build a protein.

concepts.title: Mutations
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Mutations are changes in DNA sequence, such as substitutions, insertions, and deletions, that can alter the protein a gene encodes.

concepts.title: Biotechnology
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Biotechnology tools such as PCR, gel electrophoresis, and genetic engineering let scientists copy, separate, and modify DNA.

concepts.title: Natural selection
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Natural selection is the process by which heritable traits that improve survival and reproduction become more common in a population over generations.

concepts.title: Evidence for evolution
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Fossils, homologous structures, embryology, and molecular DNA comparisons provide converging lines of evidence for common descent.

concepts.title: Speciation
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Speciation is the formation of new species when populations become reproductively isolated and diverge genetically over time.

concepts.title: Phylogenetic trees
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Phylogenetic trees are branching diagrams that represent evolutionary relationships and common ancestry among groups of organisms.

concepts.title: Population growth
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Populations grow exponentially with unlimited resources but follow logistic growth toward carrying capacity as resources become limited.

concepts.title: Energy flow in ecosystems
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Energy flows one way through food chains and food webs, from producers to consumers, with energy lost at each trophic level shown in energy pyramids.

concepts.title: Biogeochemical cycles
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Matter such as carbon, nitrogen, and water cycles repeatedly between living organisms and the physical environment.

concepts.title: Ecological interactions
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Species interact through competition, predation, and symbiosis, and these relationships shape community structure and population sizes.

concepts.title: Human impact on ecosystems
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Human activities such as climate change, habitat destruction, and invasive species disrupt ecosystems and reduce biodiversity.

concepts.title: Properties of water
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Water's polarity and hydrogen bonding give rise to cohesion, adhesion, high specific heat, and its role as the universal biological solvent.

concepts.title: Macromolecule synthesis and hydrolysis
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Biological polymers are built by dehydration synthesis and broken down by hydrolysis, the directional reactions that assemble and disassemble macromolecules.

concepts.title: Nucleic acid structure
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: DNA and RNA are nucleotide polymers with directional sugar-phosphate backbones; their antiparallel structure and base pairing underlie information storage.

concepts.title: Levels of protein structure
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary structure determine protein shape and function, with R-group interactions driving folding and denaturation.

concepts.title: Organelle structure and function
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Eukaryotic organelles each have a structure suited to their function, with the endomembrane system coordinating protein synthesis, modification, and transport.

concepts.title: Cell size and surface area-to-volume ratio
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: As cells grow, volume increases faster than surface area, limiting cell size because exchange of materials across the membrane cannot keep up.

concepts.title: Fluid mosaic membrane model
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The membrane is a dynamic fluid mosaic of phospholipids, cholesterol, and embedded proteins whose composition controls fluidity and selective permeability.

concepts.title: Membrane transport mechanisms
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Passive transport, facilitated diffusion, active transport, and bulk transport move substances across membranes, with active processes requiring energy.

concepts.title: Water potential and tonicity
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Water potential combines solute and pressure potential to predict the direction of water movement across membranes in hypertonic, hypotonic, and isotonic conditions.

concepts.title: Enzyme kinetics and regulation
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Enzyme reaction rates depend on substrate concentration, temperature, and pH, and are tuned by competitive and noncompetitive inhibition and allosteric regulation.

concepts.title: Free energy and metabolism
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Cells obey thermodynamics, coupling exergonic and endergonic reactions through ATP so that energetically unfavorable processes can proceed.

concepts.title: Glycolysis
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Glycolysis splits glucose into two pyruvate molecules in the cytoplasm, producing a small net yield of ATP and NADH.

concepts.title: The Krebs cycle
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The Krebs cycle oxidizes acetyl-CoA in the mitochondrial matrix, releasing carbon dioxide and storing energy in NADH and FADH2.

concepts.title: Electron transport chain and oxidative phosphorylation
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The electron transport chain pumps protons across the inner mitochondrial membrane, and ATP synthase uses the resulting gradient to drive chemiosmotic ATP synthesis.

concepts.title: Photosynthesis: light-dependent reactions
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: In the thylakoid membrane, photosystems capture light to split water, generating ATP and NADPH through photophosphorylation.

concepts.title: The Calvin cycle
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The Calvin cycle uses ATP and NADPH to fix carbon dioxide into sugar in the stroma, with rubisco catalyzing the initial carbon fixation step.

concepts.title: Cell signaling pathways
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Cells communicate through reception, transduction, and response, with signal molecules binding receptors to trigger intracellular signaling cascades.

concepts.title: Feedback mechanisms
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Positive and negative feedback loops regulate biological systems, maintaining homeostasis or amplifying responses as needed.

concepts.title: Cell cycle regulation
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Checkpoints, cyclins, and cyclin-dependent kinases control progression through the cell cycle, and their failure can lead to uncontrolled division and cancer.

concepts.title: Meiosis and genetic diversity
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Meiosis generates genetic diversity through crossing over, independent assortment, and random fertilization, producing variation among offspring.

concepts.title: Mendelian genetics and probability
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The laws of segregation and independent assortment, combined with probability rules and dihybrid crosses, predict inheritance across multiple genes.

concepts.title: Non-Mendelian and chromosomal inheritance
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Linkage, sex linkage, epistasis, and cytoplasmic inheritance produce ratios that depart from simple Mendelian expectations.

concepts.title: Chromosomal basis of inheritance
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Genes reside on chromosomes, and errors such as nondisjunction during meiosis lead to chromosomal disorders and aneuploidy.

concepts.title: DNA replication machinery
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Semiconservative replication uses helicase, primase, DNA polymerase, and ligase to copy leading and lagging strands at the replication fork.

concepts.title: Transcription and RNA processing
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: RNA polymerase transcribes DNA into pre-mRNA, which is processed by 5' capping, polyadenylation, and splicing to remove introns.

concepts.title: Translation and the genetic code
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Ribosomes read mRNA codons and tRNA delivers matching amino acids during initiation, elongation, and termination to build a polypeptide.

concepts.title: Regulation of gene expression
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Prokaryotic operons and eukaryotic regulatory elements, transcription factors, and epigenetic modifications control when and how much a gene is expressed.

concepts.title: Mutations and genetic variation
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Point mutations, frameshifts, and chromosomal changes alter the genome and are a major source of heritable variation acted on by evolution.

concepts.title: Biotechnology techniques
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: PCR, gel electrophoresis, DNA sequencing, and cloning let scientists amplify, separate, read, and manipulate genetic material.

concepts.title: Mechanisms of natural selection
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Directional, stabilizing, and disruptive selection shift trait distributions in populations, alongside sexual selection and artificial selection.

concepts.title: Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: The Hardy-Weinberg model predicts allele and genotype frequencies in a non-evolving population, providing a null hypothesis against which to detect evolution.

concepts.title: Evidence and patterns of evolution
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Biogeography, fossils, comparative anatomy, and molecular data document evolutionary change and support common ancestry.

concepts.title: Speciation and phylogenetic trees
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Allopatric and sympatric speciation generate new species, and phylogenetic trees and cladograms reconstruct evolutionary relationships from shared characters.

concepts.title: Origin of life
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Hypotheses for the origin of life, including abiotic synthesis of organic molecules and the RNA world, explain how early self-replicating systems could arise.

concepts.title: Population ecology and growth models
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Exponential and logistic growth models, along with density-dependent and density-independent factors, describe how populations change over time.

concepts.title: Energy flow and productivity
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Energy enters ecosystems through primary production and flows through trophic levels with roughly 10 percent efficiency, shaping food web structure.

concepts.title: Community ecology
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Species interactions such as competition, predation, and symbiosis, along with succession, structure communities and drive changes in biodiversity over time.

concepts.title: Biogeochemical cycles and disruptions
concepts.subject: biology
concepts.description: Carbon, nitrogen, and phosphorus cycle through ecosystems, and human disruptions to these cycles drive climate change and ecosystem imbalance.
